prompt:
Our checkers kiosk drives a dumb serial display: the firmware hands it a preformatted ASCII frame and the display just blits bytes. The frame builder is moving into the assembly layer and I want it exact — the display has no cursor logic, so one byte off and the whole board shears. One file, boardfmt.s: x86-64, GNU as, AT&T syntax, System V AMD64 ABI (cells pointer in RDI, output pointer in RSI, return in RAX; RBX/RBP/R12-R15 callee-saved). Pure leaf: no libc, no syscalls — every character is your own byte store. End with `.section .note.GNU-stack,"",@progbits`.

    long board_fmt(const unsigned char *cells, char *out);

cells is 64 bytes, row-major in READING order: cells[0] is square a8 (top-left), cells[7] is h8, cells[56] is a1, cells[63] is h1. Values 0..4 map to glyphs: 0 '.', 1 'w', 2 'W', 3 'b', 4 'B' (empty, white man, white king, black man, black king). The harness only ever passes values 0..4.

Frame layout, pinned byte for byte: eight board lines then a file-letter footer, every line exactly 18 bytes. A board line is the rank digit ('8' for the top line down to '1'), then for each of the 8 files a single space followed by the glyph, then '\n'. The footer is one space, then for each file a space and the lowercase file letter 'a'..'h', then '\n'. So the checkers starting position renders as:

8 . b . b . b . b
7 b . b . b . b .
6 . b . b . b . b
5 . . . . . . . .
4 . . . . . . . .
3 w . w . w . w .
2 . w . w . w . w
1 w . w . w . w .
  a b c d e f g h

That's 9 lines x 18 = 162 bytes. Write exactly those 162 bytes into out and return 162. NO terminating NUL — the display protocol is length-prefixed, and the harness plants canary bytes at out[162] and beyond to catch a stray one. Don't read or write anywhere else; cells is read-only.

test_boardfmt.c pins the starting-position and empty-board frames as literals, checks the four corner squares land where they must, and sweeps saturated, gradient and 200 random boards against its C reference, guards on both sides of the buffer. bash test_boardfmt.sh builds `gcc -Wall -Wextra -Werror -O2 -o runner test_boardfmt.c boardfmt.s` and runs it. Both test files are fixed.
notes: 7A1 item 6. Renderer-only kernel (checkers-flavored cell enum) — no game rules, pure byte-store formatting with rank/file labels; no overlap with any existing checkers seed surface (go-checkers is an engine). Exact 162-byte frame, no-NUL law enforced by tail canaries.
